mnemonics.title: Green IT Pillars
mnemonics.content: D.E.R. - Design (eco-friendly products), Energy (efficiency), Recycling (e-waste management)

mnemonics.title: 3 R's Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: R-R-R: Reduce energy use → Reuse hardware → Recycle e-waste

mnemonics.title: Green Computing Strategies
mnemonics.content: V.C.T.S. - Virtualization, Cloud computing, Thin clients, Sleep mode

acronyms.usage: Measures data center energy efficiency (Total Facility Energy / IT Equipment Energy)
Organizations' ethical contribution to sustainable development
EU directive for e-waste collection and recycling
Inverse of PUE, measures IT equipment efficiency (IT Equipment Energy / Total Facility Energy)
Emissions contributing to climate change, measured in carbon footprint

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pizza (total energy). The inner circle is IT equipment energy, outer ring is cooling/overhead. Smaller outer ring = lower PUE = greener data center. Draw a target: center = 1.0 (perfect), outer rings showing 1.5, 2.0 (inefficient).

visualTips.visualize: A pyramid with 'Reduce' at top (most important), 'Reuse' in middle, 'Recycle' at base. This shows priority order for environmental impact.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a building with arrows: Electricity in → Split: Some to IT (useful), Most to Cooling/Overhead (waste). Show PUE as fraction: PUE = Total/IT. Lower cooling = better PUE.

analogies.analogy: Like one big apartment building (physical server) housing many families (virtual machines) instead of many small houses (individual servers), saving resources and space.
Like a post office box - you don't need a whole house (full PC), just a small terminal to access services provided centrally (server).
Like fuel efficiency in a car. Lower PUE (like higher MPG) means less energy 'fuel' wasted for the same 'distance' (computing work).
Like toxic household waste but for electronics - just as you shouldn't pour poison down the drain, you shouldn't dump electronics in landfills.
